refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and end-to-end scope, focusing on measurable performance for safety-critical teleoperation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under agreed network conditions using telemetry logs and network monitoring tools.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator command input and vehicle response.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize network protocol stack and compression algorithms to reduce latency.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network infrastructure and QoS prioritization.
DP3: Implement edge computing nodes to process commands closer to the vehicle.
DP4: Design telemetry data aggregation and filtering to minimize data volume.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network infrastructure and QoS prioritization.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network infrastructure and vehicle communication modules.
Dependency on external network conditions and QoS enforcement.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency reduction to network infrastructure improvements, minimizing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of network infrastructure supporting QoS and low latency.
Risk of network outages or degradation impacting latency beyond design parameters.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP3) if network QoS alone is insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation communication, including network link management, telemetry data handling, and command tasking.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical and logical network infrastructure enabling data exchange between operator and vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time vehicle status and sensor data feedback to the operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands for vehicle control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope; low containment specificity for teleoperation latency.
Vehicle module handles physical actuation but not network latency control.
Highest containment score; directly responsible for teleoperation communication and latency.
Supports safety alerts but not primary latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control is best contained within the Teleoperation module, which manages communication protocols and network interfaces.
containment_risks: Potential diffusion if latency-related logic is duplicated in Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules.
Dependency on external network infrastructure outside module control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure strict interface definitions and avoid latency logic duplication across modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (network infrastructure and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR of latency control with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Risk of latency logic duplication in Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules.
Dependency on external network conditions may affect containment.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and clear interface boundaries, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network monitoring at the Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate diffusion risks by enforcing single ownership of latency logic in Teleoperation.
Consider fallback or redundancy strategies in Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns a clear IM boundary with manageable risks and verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator command input and vehicle response.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator within latency constraints.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS and low latency.
Telemetry data can be reliably collected and analyzed.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of latency logic across modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational network conditions.
Verification of interface adherence and module ownership.
